text: Morning Jarvis. What's my agenda for this Monday?
title: Morning Greeting

text: Morning Mike! Today is Monday, September 16. You have a 1:1 with Anika at 2 PM, and Ethan has piano with Mrs. Patterson.
title: Jarvis Morning Briefing

text: Hey Jarvis, Researching rover power systems in depth. Building humidity sensors for the greenhouse. My ADHD hyperfocus really kicked in.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Understood. Space science and tech is a great focus. ESP32 sensor soldering is a fun project. I can fetch pinout diagrams. Let me know if you want to run testing scripts.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Quick update: Bedtime went very smoothly tonight. She was throwing broccoli on the floor demanding fruit snacks. Parenting is a constant learning process.
title: Parenting Update

text: Logged that. Toddlers are full of energy. Toddler nutrition is a constant negotiation. Blending veggies into sauce works well. Rest well tonight.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Quick log: Took Lily to her medical checkup. Ethan's grass allergies are acting up again. Hope the allergies calm down.
title: Health Update

text: Interesting. Great consistency with running. I've set a calendar slot for the clinic and added meds refills. Keep stretching that knee!
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Quick update: Updating our financial spreadsheets. Researching the tax benefits of the Texas 529 plan. Will talk this over with Sarah.
title: Finance Update

text: Got it, Mike. Budget tracking is updated. 529 plans offer great tax-free growth. I've archived the plan details. I'll update the budget sheet.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Hey Jarvis, Ethan is working on a school assignment at the table. He is asking lots of questions about exoplanet atmospheric compositions. He is really putting in the effort.
title: Kids School Update

text: Logged that. That's wonderful. That's a great interest. I can summarize the atmosphere of Venus in a kid-friendly way. Let me know if he needs any more help.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Jarvis, Analyzing the system logs from yesterday's release. We are hitting major bottleneck issues with /user/verify under load. I think we need a compromise before we commit.
title: Work Update

text: Got it, Mike. I can help with that. We should implement a connection pool or check the indexing on the underlying tables for /user/verify. Should we schedule a calendar slot to do a deep dive?
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Just wanted to mention that anika and I were mapping out the sprint goals. The database query on the patient onboarding schema is running very slow. Greg is not going to like this, but we have to push back.
title: Work Update

text: That is a common bottleneck. I recommend analyzing the query execution plan to see if a compound index can speed up patient onboarding. I'll save this reasoning in our engineering scratchpad.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Quick update: Reading scientific papers late tonight. Wrote a vector search indexing script in Go. Love going down these rabbit holes.
title: Neurodivergent Update

text: Got it, Mike. I checked the telemetry feeds. Go vector search tools are speedy. Let me know if you want validation runs. Let me know if you want to run testing scripts.
title: Jarvis Neurodivergent Response

text: Just wanted to mention that bedtime went very smoothly tonight. We discussed positive discipline strategies for toddler tantrums. We need to stay consistent with them.
title: Parenting Update

text: Interesting. Screen time is always a negotiation. Consistency is key. Establishing clear, warm boundaries is highly effective. I can set a Roblox timer if needed.
title: Jarvis Parenting Response

text: Hey Jarvis, Tracking my weekly fitness metrics. Ethan's grass allergies are acting up again. Need to focus on recovery.
title: Health Update

text: Sure thing! Sleep quality is so important. I've set a calendar slot for the clinic and added meds refills. Keep stretching that knee!
title: Jarvis Health Response

text: Quick update: Looking over our mortgage options. The refi quote saves us about $250 a month. Will talk this over with Sarah.
title: Finance Update

text: Got it, Mike. Budget tracking is updated. That's $3,000 annually. It could cover the Colorado trip or 529 plans. I've saved the 529 details.
title: Jarvis Finance Response

text: Quick log: Ethan came home from Liberty Elementary in a great mood. We need to sign his permission slip for the field trip to the science museum. He is really putting in the effort.
title: Kids School Update

text: School projects are fun. I've added the deadline and details to the calendar so we don't forget. Let me know if he needs any more help.
title: Jarvis Kids School Response

text: Jarvis, Writing up user stories for our upcoming sprint. Anika Patel did a deep competitor analysis showing MyChart has secure messaging. We need to back this up with solid performance metrics.
title: Work Update

text: Let's break this down. This competitor data is a powerful lever. You can use it to justify keeping secure messaging in the MVP. Should we schedule a calendar slot to do a deep dive?
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Anika and I were mapping out the sprint goals. We got beta feedback from clinics suggesting they need secure messaging immediately. We need to get this sorted before the Baylor pilot starts.
title: Work Update

text: Sure thing! Understood. We should prioritize these clinic requests. Let's create a dedicated sub-task in the CareConnect backlog. I can add this optimization to the backlog.
title: Jarvis Work Response

text: Time to log today's thoughts, Jarvis. Made good progress on my work tasks today. CareConnect prep is moving along. Tuned my local LLM settings and fits scrapers. Ready for sleep.
title: Evening Journal Entry

text: Journal summary recorded. Have a peaceful night.
title: Jarvis Journal Summary
